preamble.authority_statement: By the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, it is hereby ordered as follows:
preamble.introductory_text: The United States faces persistent and increasingly sophisticated malicious cyber campaigns that threaten the public sector, the private sector, and the American people’s security and privacy. The Federal Government must improve its efforts to identify, deter, protect against, detect, and respond to these actions and actors. Cybersecurity requires partnership with the private sector, investment in bold changes, and trustworthy digital infrastructure.

purpose.statement: To improve the cybersecurity of Federal Information Systems, enhance software supply chain security, establish a Cyber Safety Review Board, and modernize Federal cybersecurity practices.

policy.statement: The prevention, detection, assessment, and remediation of cyber incidents is a top priority and essential to national and economic security. The Federal Government must lead by example, ensuring all Federal Information Systems meet or exceed the cybersecurity standards set forth in this order.

definitions.definition: A coordinated cybersecurity strategy that eliminates implicit trust and continuously verifies users and devices.
A formal record containing the details and supply chain relationships of components used in building software.

directives.text: The Federal Government must make bold changes and significant investments to defend the vital institutions that underpin the American way of life.

sections.section_title: Removing Barriers to Sharing Threat Information

directives.text: Within 60 days, OMB shall review FAR and DFARS language for contracting with IT and OT service providers and recommend updates to improve information sharing.
Service providers must collect and preserve data relevant to cybersecurity events and share them with appropriate agencies.
Within 60 days, each agency shall develop a plan to implement Zero Trust Architecture and provide reports to OMB and APNSA.
Within 180 days, agencies shall adopt multi-factor authentication and encryption for data at rest and in transit.
Within 30 days, NIST shall solicit input from stakeholders to develop standards and practices for software supply chain security.
Within 45 days, NIST shall publish a definition of 'critical software.'

sections.section_title: Establishing a Cyber Safety Review Board

directives.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security, in consultation with the Attorney General, shall establish the Cyber Safety Review Board under Section 871 of the Homeland Security Act.

sections.section_title: Standardizing Federal Playbook for Cyber Response

directives.text: Within 120 days, DHS through CISA shall develop a standardized playbook for cybersecurity vulnerability and incident response for Federal Civilian Executive Branch systems.
Federal agencies shall deploy an Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) initiative to support proactive detection and incident response.
Within 14 days, DHS shall provide OMB recommendations for logging requirements, retention, and protection of system and network data.
Within 60 days, the Secretary of Defense, in coordination with DNI and CNSS, shall adopt cybersecurity requirements for National Security Systems equivalent to or exceeding this order.

implementation.instructions: Agencies must work with DHS, CISA, OMB, and other designated entities to implement directives on threat information sharing, Zero Trust Architecture, software supply chain security, and incident response.

general_provisions.legal_authority: This order is issued pursuant to the Constitution and laws of the United States, including Section 1752 of Public Law 116-283.
general_provisions.non_impairment_clause: Nothing in this order impairs the authority of any agency or official granted by law.
general_provisions.enforceability_clause: This order does not create any right or benefit, substantive or procedural, enforceable by law or equity by any party.
